title: lesquerella kingii subsp. bernardina
intro: Lesquerella kingii subsp. bernardina, also known as California bladderpod, is a rare and endangered succulent plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to inhibit the growth of certain types of cancer cells and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Lesquerella kingii subsp. bernardina include its potential to reduce inflammation, fight cancer, and act as an antioxidant. Traditionally, the plant is prepared for herbal use by making a tea from its roots and leaves, or by infusing it in oil for topical applications, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits.
